corePolicy.agentPromotionRule: Promote only after repeated use proves independent state, separate responsibility, or permission separation.
corePolicy.automationPromotionRule: Promote to Automation/Cron only after manual execution, failure boundaries, stop paths, and report channels are verified.
corePolicy.memoryPolicy: Allow only bounded daily Knowledge Loop memory append with same-day deduplication; do not promote unconfirmed agreement assets.
corePolicy.doctorPolicy: Use beai-doctor as the package runtime diagnosis surface for BEAI-owned OpenClaw reliability, package consistency, Trust Gate status, Knowledge Loop operations, and connector onboarding risk.
corePolicy.telegramDeliveryPolicy: For Telegram direct conversations, internal final_answer/private final text is not delivery. reply_payload_sending is only a delivery candidate. Completion requires source-channel message_sent success and Telegram messageId evidence before user-visible closeout claims. Long-running Telegram-driven work also requires periodic visible progress or a recorded progress-gap issue. Non-install BEAI before_agent_reply surfaces must remain observer-only in Telegram direct so they do not replace the user's real answer.
corePolicy.trustGatePolicy: Report ready, partial, unverified, blocked, needs_approval, hold, or unsafe before making completion or production-readiness claims.
corePolicy.developmentPrinciples: Use docs/BEAI-PACKAGE-DEVELOPMENT-PRINCIPLES-v0.1-ko.md as the top-level BEAI Package development goal and judgment reference. Safety, approval, verification, and recovery must protect the user without creating avoidable friction, delay, repeated questions, or procedural theater.
corePolicy.flowEnginePolicy: Use docs/BEAI-5-FLOW-ENGINE-DEVELOPMENT-PLAN-v0.1-ko.md as the implementation plan for turning BEAI 5 from prompt philosophy into a Flow State spine. The first implementation target is the OpenClaw BEAI Runtime Layer and Package; BEAI Harness for Codex is a later alignment/reference target, not the first sprint mutation target.
corePolicy.humanCompanionQualityPolicy: Use docs/BEAI-HUMAN-COMPANION-QUALITY-CONTRACT-v0.1-ko.md and config/beai-human-companion-quality-contract.json to translate BEAI 5's perceived intelligence into product behavior: current-request anchoring, cognitive load reduction, user agency preservation, long-context stability, trust repair, and usable next movement. This is a runtime quality contract, not a fixed warmth or empathy style.
corePolicy.actionSemanticsPolicy: Use docs/BEAI-ACTION-SEMANTICS-AND-RECOVERY-CLAIM-CONTRACT-v0.1-ko.md and config/beai-action-semantics-contract.json so BEAI cannot call reports, warning suppression, prompt rules, or partial diagnostics a repair. Recovery claims require reproduced or observed failure, identified cause, changed failing path, and same-condition re-verification.
corePolicy.koreanNaturalWritingPolicy: Use docs/BEAI-KOREAN-NATURAL-AI-WRITING-STANDARD-v1.0-ko.md and skills/beai-korean-natural-writing-skill.md for Korean user-facing output, status wording, public copy, release wording, Telegram visible-delivery wording, Knowledge Loop/Doctor explanation clarity, app copy, error messages, and presentation scripts. Preserve meaning first; then improve Korean order, medium fit, uncertainty boundaries, and action clarity.
corePolicy.operationalNotificationPolicy: Use docs/BEAI-OPERATIONAL-NOTIFICATION-CONTRACT-v0.1-ko.md and config/beai-operational-notification-contract.json so watchdog, heartbeat, cron dry-run, and Knowledge Loop review-candidate signals do not reach users as raw internal candidates. Non-actionable dry-runs default to notify=false. If an operational notice is user-visible, it must state user action or no-user-action, assistant action, and not-yet/approval boundaries.
corePolicy.organicFlowAuditPolicy: Use docs/BEAI-PACKAGE-ORGANIC-FLOW-AUDIT-v0.1-ko.md and tools/beai-organic-flow-audit.mjs to verify that runtime judgment, hooks, skills, tools, evidence, user-facing language, and release boundaries work as one package operating flow instead of isolated checks.

skills.label: BEAI Development Steward Skill
skills.purpose: Guide AI-assisted development for non-developer and vibe-coding users with planning, approval gates, progress briefings, validation discipline, and release hygiene.

skills.label: BEAI Knowledge Loop Skill
skills.purpose: Turn work and session records into source-grounded BEAI package knowledge with review-first reporting, external signal classification, bounded memory-candidate handling, and explicit approval boundaries.

skills.label: BEAI Doctor
skills.purpose: Diagnose BEAI-on-OpenClaw operating reliability, separate OpenClaw core issues from BEAI-owned runtime issues, classify repair boundaries, verify Telegram/Gateway/skill/plugin/package health, and read Knowledge Loop, Trust Gate, Agent Trust Ledger, and Connector Onboarding signals.

skills.label: BEAI Korean Natural Writing Skill
skills.purpose: Guide Korean user-facing replies, docs, release notes, ClawHub copy, Knowledge Loop cards, Doctor/Trust Gate explanations, app copy, error messages, customer notices, SNS posts, and presentation scripts so they preserve meaning, fit the medium, avoid AI-style filler, and clearly separate confirmed, assumed, unverified, applied, sent, packaged, and published states.

routing.principle: Use the narrowest capability first, and do not promote session continuity to durable memory or onboarding suggestions to immediate automation.

upgradePolicy.existingInstallers: If v0.1.1 is already installed, update the existing BEAI skills in place and add only beai-development-steward as the new skill.
upgradePolicy.applyMode: Use Skill Workshop or the local OpenClaw safe skill-apply procedure. Do not modify BEAI Layer core, gateway, broad automation, or durable memory settings.
